title: Raccoon Removal in Fordham, Bronx | $900-$4,000 | Best Pest Control NYC
metaDescription: Professional raccoon removal services in Fordham, Bronx. Licensed experts serving the Bronx Zoo area, Fordham University campus, and surrounding neighborhoods. Call for fast, humane raccoon control.
intro: Raccoons are increasingly common in Fordham, taking advantage of the neighborhood's mix of residential buildings, green spaces near Fordham University, and access to the Bronx River. These intelligent animals often invade attics, basements, and garages, causing property damage and health risks. Best Pest Control NYC provides professional, humane raccoon removal services throughout Fordham and the greater Bronx area.
whyUs: Our licensed wildlife specialists have over 15 years of experience handling raccoon infestations in Fordham's diverse housing stock, from classic Bronx walk-ups to single-family homes near the Botanical Garden. We provide same-day emergency service, humane trapping and relocation, complete exclusion work to prevent re-entry, and full cleanup and sanitization of affected areas.
localContext: Fordham's proximity to the New York Botanical Garden and Bronx Zoo creates ideal conditions for raccoon activity, with wildlife frequently moving through residential areas along Webster Avenue and Fordham Road. The neighborhood's older brick buildings and multi-family homes often have structural vulnerabilities that raccoons exploit to gain entry into attics and crawl spaces.

faqs.question: How much does raccoon removal cost in Fordham?
faqs.answer: Raccoon removal in Fordham typically costs between $900 and $4,000, depending on the severity of the infestation, number of raccoons, accessibility of the location, and extent of exclusion work needed. A single raccoon in an accessible attic may cost $900-$1,500, while a family of raccoons with extensive damage and entry point sealing can reach $3,000-$4,000. We provide free inspections and detailed quotes before beginning work.

faqs.question: Are raccoons common in the Fordham area?
faqs.answer: Yes, raccoons are very common in Fordham due to the neighborhood's proximity to green spaces like the Bronx Botanical Garden, Bronx Zoo, and Bronx Park. These areas provide natural habitat that raccoons expand from into residential neighborhoods. The abundance of food sources from garbage bins along busy streets like Fordham Road and the older building stock with accessible entry points make Fordham particularly attractive to raccoons.

faqs.question: How quickly can you remove raccoons from my Fordham property?
faqs.answer: We offer same-day emergency service for Fordham residents and can typically begin the removal process within 24 hours of your call. The complete removal timeline depends on the situation: a single raccoon may be trapped within 1-3 days, while a mother with babies may take 1-2 weeks to ensure all animals are safely removed. We prioritize humane methods and follow all NYC wildlife regulations throughout the process.

faqs.question: What damage can raccoons cause to Fordham homes?
faqs.answer: Raccoons can cause significant damage to Fordham properties, including torn roof shingles, damaged insulation, chewed electrical wiring (fire hazard), destroyed ductwork, and contaminated attics with feces and urine. They often tear open soffits, vents, and fascia boards to gain entry. Their waste can carry diseases like raccoon roundworm and leptospirosis, making professional cleanup essential. We provide complete damage assessment, repairs, and sanitization as part of our comprehensive raccoon removal service.
